inc()[source]#
class ThreadPool[source]#

Bases: QThreadPool

Extended from QThreadPool class.

createWorker(fn, *args)[source]#

Create and return a worker object.

Params:

fn (function): the function for the worker to run *args: the args to be passed into the function

staticMetaObject = PySide6.QtCore.QMetaObject("ThreadPool" inherits "QThreadPool": )#
class ThreadPoolProgBar[source]#

Bases: ThreadPool

startAll(text='', status_bar=None)[source]#
staticMetaObject = PySide6.QtCore.QMetaObject("ThreadPoolProgBar" inherits "ThreadPool": )#
class Worker(fn, *args)[source]#

Bases: QRunnable, QObject

Worker thread

Inherits from QRunnable to handler worker thread setup, signals and wrap-up.

Parameters:

  • callback (function) – The function callback to run on this worker thread. Supplied args and kwargs will be passed through to the runner.

  • args – Arguments to pass to the callback function

  • kwargs – Keywords to pass to the callback function

run()[source]#

Initialise the runner function with passed args.

staticMetaObject = PySide6.QtCore.QMetaObject("Worker" inherits "QObject": Methods:   #5 type=Slot, signature=run() )#
class WorkerSignals[source]#

Bases: QObject

Defines the signals available from a running worker thread.

Supported signals are:

finished

No data

error

tuple (exctype, value, traceback.format_exc() )

result

object data returned from processing, anything

progress

int indicating % progress

error#
finished#
result#
staticMetaObject = PySide6.QtCore.QMetaObject("WorkerSignals" inherits "QObject": Methods:   #5 type=Signal, signature=error(PyObject), parameters=PyObject   #6 type=Signal, signature=result(PyObject), parameters=PyObject   #7 type=Signal, signature=finished() )#
